124957.explanation: The SGP is not in favor. You elect council members to make sensible decisions on your behalf. A referendum is often too simplistic (yes/no). Moreover, the result often leads to disappointment because explanations can be given afterward. That is pseudo-participation and undermines trust.

124958.explanation: The SGP supports citizen initiatives. The park at de Noord is a wonderful example of successful green management by residents. For real estate such as sports halls, a foundation or association can play a role. Tailored solutions are therefore necessary.

124961.explanation: The SGP encourages local initiatives. If residents take joint responsibility for sustainability (stewardship), the municipality may certainly support that. It strengthens society. However, financial support must responsibly fit within the budget.

124962.explanation: Space is scarce. We reject large-scale 'block box' webshops that add little value. But for logistics that strengthen our maritime sector and fish auction, like the new Seafood hub, there is room. Quality, employment, and strengthening the local economy take priority over square meters.

124967.explanation: Given the nuisance during church services on New Year’s Eve, a central show can contribute to public order, safety, and atmosphere. However, the SGP does not believe the Municipality of Urk should pay for this. Regarding a fireworks ban, enforcement seems difficult.

124968.explanation: Nothing is free; otherwise, waste disposal charges increase for everyone. The principle 'the polluter pays' is fairer. However, bringing waste to the recycling center must remain accessible to prevent dumping in nature. More (extended) opening hours are needed.

124970.explanation: On Urk, everyone counts. The costs of structural modifications do not seem an obstacle for Urk businesses. Appreciation from the municipality in the form of a prize or gift is a nice way to encourage good employment practices for the disabled and elderly.

124971.explanation: Given the housing shortage, we must be creative. If farmers can build homes on their own land (e.g., for family), this relieves the market. This must be well integrated into the landscape ('red for red').

124972.explanation: Sport unites people and is healthy. Financial barriers must not prevent participation, especially for children from low-income families. Support from the Municipality of Urk is not necessary. Urk associations and clubs can arrange this themselves through their membership fees.

124973.explanation: The local broadcaster Urk FM is widely appreciated. The municipality supports Urk FM where possible, including through the issuance of broadcasting permits. Support in Urk does not always have to be financial. Much can also be achieved through sponsorships and donations.

124980.explanation: Safety at home is crucial; domestic violence must stop. However, shelters require anonymity, which is difficult in a close-knit community like Urk. Regional shelters are often safer, provided the threshold to seek help is low.

124983.explanation: The municipality is legally not allowed to refuse registration in the BRP if someone declares a recreational home as their actual residence, but it is generally obliged to enforce the zoning plan. Given the housing shortage, bespoke solutions for urgent cases are just and fair.

124986.explanation: Living is becoming more expensive. Even working poor people are struggling. By raising the threshold (e.g., to 120% of the social minimum), we prevent people from falling through the cracks. Poverty reduction must be generous. Note: answer choice due to poorly formulated statement: level B1+/B2.

124988.explanation: Electric driving is increasing, so charging poles are necessary. But this must not come at the expense of the already scarce parking capacity in neighborhoods. Smart solutions and dual use are needed, not a blind urge that 'consumes' parking spaces.

124989.explanation: Safety in residential areas must come first, especially with many children playing. 30 km/h and elements paving are the norm there. On through roads with asphalt paving, 50 km/h should remain possible for good traffic flow. This is the situation in Urk.

124990.explanation: Care touches the heart of life. It is essential that residents can receive care that aligns with their identity and beliefs. Identity-based care and freedom of choice are non-negotiable for the SGP.

124993.explanation: Dog ownership incurs costs for the municipality (cleaning, enforcement). It is reasonable that the owner contributes to this via a targeted tax. However, the tax must be proportional to the services provided.

124994.explanation: Tourism brings crowds and costs for the municipality. It is fair that visitors make a proper contribution to the maintenance of our beautiful village. This relieves the local residents. Every year we increase the tourist tax.

124996.explanation: The housing shortage among local residents is large. Priority rules promote displacement and undermine support. We advocate allocation based on registration duration. No automatic priority for status holders.

125003.explanation: Space in Urk is scarce; high-rise buildings offer a solution to address the housing shortage. Higher buildings could be possible at the Noorderzand location. From there you have a beautiful view of the IJsselmeer and the forest. However, this must fit the scale and character of the village and the skyline with the lighthouse.

125004.explanation: A total ban goes too far. We do exclude commercial rentals that cause nuisance; houses are not cash cows. But living with young couples with family or hospitation renting for students (Firda) must remain possible. In the housing shortage, helping each other is a great good.

125007.explanation: Urk already has an enormous housing shortage itself. Housing migrant workers is primarily a task for employers and employment agencies, preferably in locations (e.g., a campus on the outskirts) that do not put pressure on the regular starter housing market.

125008.explanation: It is still questionable whether paid parking is a solution to the problem in Old Urk. In addition, the business model must first be investigated. The question is whether the costs of paid parking outweigh the benefits. Implementation, management, and maintenance of systems cost a lot of money.

125009.explanation: Going out must be safe and enjoyable. Those who misbehave spoil it for others. A Collective Hospitality Ban (CHO) is a powerful tool to tackle troublemakers and maintain peace on ‘the hill’.

124957.explanation: It undermines representative democracy. Important issues are often complex and not suited for a yes-no answer.

124958.explanation: It is great if participation takes place from society, but when there is dissatisfaction with its implementation, it is difficult to address this, given that residents cannot bear this responsibility.

124961.explanation: Agree, as long as it does not undermine the business case of the Heat Network.

124962.explanation: Urk is the fish hub of Europe.

124970.explanation: The buildings for which the municipality is responsible must be made accessible to people with disabilities. Buildings of entrepreneurs are the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves; the municipality cannot financially support all of these.

124971.explanation: Infrastructure must be regulated within environmental plans. The municipality is ultimately responsible for the integral picture and must maintain control in this.

124972.explanation: The social assistance benefit must itself be sufficient to pay for a sports membership.

124973.explanation: The municipality receives an amount per resident from the national government for the local broadcaster.

124980.explanation: It is important that a women’s shelter be unknown and anonymous, safeguarding the privacy of victims. This is insufficiently feasible in Urk.

124983.explanation: There are too few homes in Urk. We know that people currently live at the Urk campsite, but according to national rules this is not allowed now. The municipality must follow national legislation. The municipality is lenient and helpful towards the campsite residents.

124988.explanation: Given the growth in the number of electric cars, this is necessary and existing policy.

124989.explanation: Our long-term vision is that on the 50 km/h roads cyclists and other vulnerable traffic participants can safely participate in traffic at a safe distance from cars.

124996.explanation: The municipality has a task set by national policy to organize this.

125003.explanation: Given the housing shortage, this must be possible in locations suitable for this purpose.

125007.explanation: The number of migrant workers cannot grow indefinitely. The economic benefits must be weighed against other interests, such as housing shortage and social cohesion. But we must bear the responsibility ourselves to house our migrant workers.

125008.explanation: This will be investigated; depending on these (financial) results, in relation to the pros and cons (revenues from tourists, costs for local residents, water effect on the surrounding environment), a choice must be made.

125009.explanation: This is existing policy of the Urk hospitality sector; we agree with that.

124957.explanation: The elections are a 'referendum' for 4 years. The council has the mandate from the voter.

124958.explanation: A sports hall is too complex. But neighborhood associations can participate more if necessary or desired.

124961.explanation: The municipality plays a facilitating role for energy cooperatives.

124962.explanation: Hardly plays a role here in Urk and is fully dependent on size.

124968.explanation: Only when it can be arranged within the current waste levy.

124970.explanation: The municipality is responsible for public spaces. Entrepreneurs can manage perfectly well themselves.

124971.explanation: The preference is for the municipality to acquire land. If that is not possible, customized solutions per location are possible.

124972.explanation: With a maximum budget per person, everyone can participate.

124973.explanation: The local broadcaster is of great importance to Urk.

124980.explanation: Not applicable to Urk. In such a house you want to shelter anonymously and secretly.

124986.explanation: Tailored solutions for people in need must always be possible.

124988.explanation: The demand from the neighborhoods is leading in this.

124989.explanation: It is actually already almost everywhere.

124990.explanation: Identity-based care is a key focus of the Christian Union.

124993.explanation: The municipality facilitates provisions for dogs.

124996.explanation: Government policy is decisive in this.

125003.explanation: Tailored solutions apply here as well: it depends on demand and location.

125004.explanation: Tailored solutions are appropriate here. Landlords should always be an option.

125007.explanation: You cannot force anyone to live somewhere.

125008.explanation: This could be possible, but the costs must not exceed the revenues and only in tourist areas.

125009.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs already do this on their own initiative.

124957.explanation: Every resident has their own voice. We find it important that this is heard and not forgotten.

124958.explanation: The municipality must have its affairs in order before responsibilities are shifted to residents. If a resident wants this, the possibility must be there.

124961.explanation: As long as it helps prevent grid congestion.

124962.explanation: Our industry has an interest in good infrastructure and distribution.

124967.explanation: With the current nationwide fireworks ban coming, the municipality must offer a good alternative.

124970.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to invest here, we should support it.

124971.explanation: There are no farms on Urk; if this were the case, we should see if it is possible.

124973.explanation: The local broadcaster is of great importance for Urk and the Urk identity.

124980.explanation: If there is demand, we must certainly realize this.

124983.explanation: If there is no supply, we must be able to house our residents; we take a nuanced view and believe it should be allowed in times of need.

124986.explanation: Residents with low incomes need support from the municipality.

124988.explanation: We need to look at this per situation; we prefer not to lose too many 'regular' parking spaces for charging spots.

124990.explanation: This is our preference for our community but more important is that we can offer care that is accessible and affordable.

124993.explanation: Life is already expensive enough for our residents; we must first look at where we can save within the municipal office.

124994.explanation: Urk has a lower tourist tax than comparable municipalities; we may allow ourselves to increase it a bit.

124996.explanation: Our own Urk residents come first.

125003.explanation: We do not want visual pollution on Urk. But if it fits within a new residential area and provides space for our starters.

125007.explanation: If there is housing, it must be realized as much as possible.

125008.explanation: We need to think carefully about this. For all of Urk it is not realistic, possibly only in the old village. If so, it must be free for our own residents and only charged for tourists.

124957.explanation: Krachtig Urk is a party that is always approachable. Top candidate Jan Hakvoort is an easily accessible person and will discuss information in the faction meetings. A referendum costs a lot of money and Krachtig Urk sees that as a waste of money.

124958.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to empower its residents. If citizens want to weed and the neighborhood association gets extra money because of it, Krachtig Urk thinks that is a good idea.

124961.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to primarily facilitate and therefore issue permits. Entrepreneurs must pay the costs themselves or apply for subsidies from RVO or the province.

124962.explanation: Krachtig Urk should facilitate entrepreneurs as much as possible.

124967.explanation: Previously, we also had a fireworks show on Urk. Krachtig Urk wants to ban fireworks. The bangs on Urk are getting louder every week and last all year round. This is terrible for our animals.

124968.explanation: Krachtig Urk knows that the municipality of Urk has little money. Free waste collection is financially not possible.

124970.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to facilitate. But every business should determine the costs themselves.

124971.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to facilitate. The municipality of Urk must maintain control.

124972.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to primarily facilitate. Citizens should pay as much as possible themselves, but there are always painful exceptions. It is better for a child to play football at SVU than to end up in expensive youth care, which our municipality also has to pay for.

124973.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the local broadcaster to be financially supported. Urk has a good local broadcaster that every Urker can listen to.

124980.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to facilitate this, but the location may also be outside Urk if that is safer.

124983.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the residents currently staying at the campsite to be allowed to stay. Where else should these people go? Sleep in the Urk forest? There are few homes available on Urk. Krachtig Urk wants a phase-out plan for the people at the Urk campsite.

124986.explanation: Krachtig Urk is a social party. Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to facilitate. Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to empower its residents and ensure citizens get a paid job. There is plenty of work on Urk.

124988.explanation: Through motions (together with other parties), Krachtig Urk has ensured that extra charging stations are installed by the swimming pool and the sports hall.

124989.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants to review this per road. Safety comes first for Krachtig Urk.

124990.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants there to be freedom of choice.

124993.explanation: Krachtig Urk believes that citizens with dogs should pay some fee.

124994.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants tourists to have an enjoyable day on Urk. Krachtig Urk wants tourists to stay longer on Urk. The Urk business community also benefits from this. Tourists already spend a lot of money on Urk, from which shops benefit.

124996.explanation: Krachtig Urk is the party that must be there for its own residents. Krachtig Urk is a Christian party and there is enough space for refugees in the polder. Urk has very little land and this land must be available for its own residents.

125003.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants taller buildings on Urk if possible. This can be done in certain places. Think of the municipal works location. If municipal works move, taller buildings could easily be built there so people have a nice view of the IJsselmeer.

125004.explanation: There is a lot of housing shortage on Urk. Krachtig Urk does not want citizens to sleep in the forest; better to stay in a single room.

125007.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants the municipality of Urk to cooperate more with neighboring municipalities. Municipality NOP has much more land than Urk. Locations can be designated where, for example, 300 labor migrants can live. Krachtig Urk wants to enable this as close to the Urk business community as possible.

125008.explanation: Krachtig Urk is the party for entrepreneurs. Krachtig Urk wants the municipality to facilitate. Krachtig Urk is the party that would like to build parking garages. These can be financed, for example, by paid parking.

125009.explanation: Krachtig Urk wants our citizens to be able to go out safely. Guests who cause nuisance should be given community service such as tidying up the parks.

124957.explanation: Agreed, in 2024 we submitted an initiative proposal to introduce a referendum regulation in the Municipality of Urk so that local referenda could be organized on Urk. Unfortunately, this was voted down by the rest of the municipal council.

124958.explanation: If, for example, a neighborhood association wants to do this, we are positive about it; however, we believe the municipality should provide compensation to neighborhood associations for this.

124961.explanation: At own choice, self-financed. If this is made compulsory by the government, then through subsidies.

124967.explanation: The municipality must offer an alternative in case of a permanent fireworks ban, partly with the help of sponsorship.

124968.explanation: May be disposed of free of charge with an environmental pass at the local processor. Just like in surrounding villages.

124970.explanation: Agreed, together with the CDA, we submitted a motion in 2025 to establish an accessibility council where people with practical experience advise the municipality about the accessibility of public spaces and buildings in our municipality.

124971.explanation: Provided it is appropriate and within the maximum number, and it does not take up valuable agricultural land. No place for asylum seekers and labor migrants.

124972.explanation: If someone with a below-average or average income has to pay this, then someone with benefits must also pay.

124973.explanation: Already receive more than enough, also considering the listener density.

124980.explanation: Even if only small-scale, provided it is financially feasible.

124986.explanation: Every help request must be assessed individually. Not everyone has the same situation. And life has become more expensive overall. Take inflation into account.

124988.explanation: New technology makes it possible to charge your car via street lamps. Research this, so it does not cost empty charging spots.

124989.explanation: Disagree, the roads in our municipality where 50 and 70 km/h are allowed are perfectly suitable to maintain the current speed limit.

124990.explanation: Care is care and is the same for everyone.

124993.explanation: Agreed, the dog tax is a nonsensical tax on Urk that flows directly into general funds and is only sporadically used for facilities for dog owners.

124994.explanation: This was recently increased and is at a good level.

124996.explanation: People who have never contributed to our society do not deserve priority. Urkers have every right to be at the top of the list.

125003.explanation: To tackle the housing shortage, this is a good solution. However, we believe it is important to carefully consider whether tall residential buildings fit within the streetscape.

125004.explanation: We do not want to create confusing situations.

125007.explanation: Not possible. A large part of the labor migrants working in Urk live in the Noordoostpolder, where there is space.

125008.explanation: Residents of our beautiful village are already burdened enough.

125009.explanation: Implement a zero tolerance policy immediately. This also applies to associations, sports clubs, and party centers.

statements.title: Guests who cause disturbances in a pub should receive a ban from all hospitality venues in Urk.
The residents of Urk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Urk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Tall apartment blocks should be permitted in Urk.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: A women's shelter should be established in Urk.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Migrant workers who work in Urk should also live in Urk.

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: die op Urk werken moeten ook op Urk wonen.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Urk should introduce paid parking.
